blocks.content: Her hand came up, brush still in it, and found the front of his jacket. Not pulling — just placed there, like an anchor.

Or she kissed him. The distinction disappeared almost immediately. It was quiet and brief and exact — the kind of kiss that isn't an experiment or a question but a statement made carefully, once, and understood completely.
When they stopped, she didn't move back. He didn't either.
She looked up at him. Her brush had left a small streak of cerulean on the left side of his jaw — she saw it and didn't say anything about it.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You have paint on your hand.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You have paint on your face.
Outside, the rain had stopped. The light through the window had shifted — later afternoon, something gold beginning in it, the clouds breaking somewhere offstage.
He thought about Mr. Alvarez and the open door.
He thought: I would do this again. All of it. Every uncertain step of it, every small decision he'd made to get here.
He thought: exactly what it needed to be.
She leaned her forehead against his.
